question:
Will Top Esports win LoL Worlds 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the esports team winning 1st place in the League of Legends 2025 Season World Championship (Worlds 2025).

If the winner of the World Cup 2025 is not determined by December 31, 2025 ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.
event_title:

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Liquipedia: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Main_Page.

evidences.title: LoL: All Worlds Winners - Sheep Esports
evidences.content: After the heartbreak of 2022, T1 entered Worlds 2023 with one mission: reclaim their throne. The new Swiss Stage format saw them steadily progress, securing a quarterfinal spot after four rounds. There, they dismantled LNG Esports with ease before taking on the tournament’s biggest favorite—JD Gaming, the LPL powerhouse chasing the Golden Road. In what became an instant classic, Faker’s legendary Azir play turned the tide of the series, and Ryu "Keria" Min-seok’s game-winning Bard performance sealed T1’s ticket to the finals, denying JDG’s perfect season. T1 accessed their second Worlds final in a row. In the Gocheok Sky Dome, T1 completed its journey dominantly, sweeping Weibo Gaming in a one-sided grand final. Faker was finally back on the top of the game, lifting his fourth Worlds [...] ## 2021: EDward Gaming For the second time in international tournaments this year, the LPL defeats the LCK in the finals, crowning EDG as champions over the powerhouse Damwon, who were making their second consecutive Worlds final. Credit: Clive Rose/Riot Games For the second time in international tournaments this year, the LPL defeats the LCK in the finals, crowning EDG as champions over the p...

evidences.content: Laugardalshöll, Reykjavik, Iceland. With Chinese cities still closed to international travel due to COVID, Worlds 2021 took place behind closed doors in Iceland — a strange, quiet edition with no live audience for the final. EDG beat DAMWON KIA 3–2 in a five-game series, handing the LPL a third title in four years. Midlaner Lee "Scout" Ye-chan took MVP. EDG 2021 ### 2022 — DRX (LCK) Top: Hwang "Kingen" Seong-hoon Jungle: Hong "Pyosik" Chang-hyeon Mid: Kim "Zeka" Geon-woo ADC: Kim "Deft" Hyuk-kyu Support: Cho "BeryL" Geon-hee [...] Pudong Football Stadium, Shanghai. Held in a pandemic-era China with a limited in-arena crowd. DAMWON beat China's Suning 3–1 to return the title to Korea after two LPL wins, with jungler Kim "Canyon" Geon-bu taking MVP. The roster became the new face of the LCK overnight and ended what had looked like the start of a sustained Chinese era. DWG 2020 ### 2021 — EDward Gaming (LPL) Top: Li "Flandre" Xuan-jun Jungle: Zhao "JieJie" Li-Jie Mid: Lee "Scout" Ye-chan ADC: Park "Viper" Do-hyeon Support: Tian "Meiko" Ye [...] Gocheok Sky Dome, Seoul. evidences.title: Sheep Esports - LoL: Top Esports completed LPL 2025 roster
evidences.content: Loading... Loading... After a rather positive year, Top Esports are making two changes to their roster for 2025. In the jungle, to compensate for Gao "Tian" Tian-Liang's departure to Weibo Gaming and for the Support role to replace Tian "Meiko" Ye, who has left for Invictus Gaming. The rest of the roster remains the same, with Lin "Creme" Jian and Yu "JackeyLove" Wen-Bo still under contract until November 2025 and toplaner Bai "369" Jia-Hao having renewed his contract earlier this offseason. ## A new Jungle/Support duo [...] ## Kanavi doesn't come alone In addition to Kanavi, TES have also recruited Yoon "Homme" Sung-young from JDG. He will officiate as Head Coach next year with a jungler he knows well, having spent almost the same number of years at JDG together. With these changes, Top Esports hope to do at least as well as last year in the LPL (2nd in Spring and 3rd in Summer), while improving their international performances, as they finished 5th-6th at MSI and 5th-8th at Worlds. On paper, the team looks set to rub shoulders with other Chinese superteams such as Invictus Gaming, JDG, BLG and WBG. ### TES 2025 roster: Top: Bai "369" Jia-Hao Jungle: Seo "Kanavi" Jin-hyeok Mid:...

evidences.title: Every League of Legends Worlds Champion 2011–2024 - YouTube
evidences.content: been doubted. They have been discounted. They have been dismissed. But DRX are your 2022 world champions. The season 13 World Championship finals took place in 2023 in Soul, South Korea, and featured a total of 22 teams. Many fans expected JDG, the LPL's top seed, to secure the title after dominant year where they won both the LPL spring and summer as well as MSI. However, the script was flipped in dramatic fashion. Korea's T1, led by Faker in his 10th World's appearance, delivered an unforgettable run that culminated in a commanding 3 to zero sweep over China's Waybo Gaming in the Grand Finals. This victory gave T1 their fourth Summoner Cup, cementing their legacy as the most successful organization in League of Legends history. The finals MVP award went to T1's top laner Zuse, whose [...] Sunning. Dom One will silence Shanghai and Dom One Gaming are your season 2020 world champions. The season 11 World Championship finals took place in 2021 in Rekavic, Iceland, featuring 24 of the world's best teams. Edward Gaming dominated the competition with standout performances from their star mid laner Scout, top laner Flandre, and AD carry Viper. In the grand finals, EDG defeated Korea'...

evidences.title: Knockout Stage Draw Show | Worlds 2025
evidences.content: # Knockout Stage Draw Show | Worlds 2025 ## LoL Esports 3930000 subscribers 563 likes ### Description 45561 views Posted: 25 Oct 2025 The Swiss Stage of Worlds 2025 is over, with eight teams advancing into the Knockout Stage. The teams are divided into three pools, based on their scorelines exiting the Swiss Stage. There are two 3-0 teams, three 3-1 teams, and three 3-2 teams. In the first draw, the two 3-0 teams will draw their opponent from the 3-2 pool, and placed on opposite sides of the bracket. In the secondary draw, the remaining 3-1 and 3-2 teams will be drawn and placed into the bracket based on the draw order. The Knockout Stage begins on October 28th. #lolesports #leagueoflegends #Worlds2025 [...] #lolesports #leagueoflegends #Worlds2025 Watch Worlds 2025 LIVE! Play-Ins: Oct 14 Swiss Stage: Oct 15 - 25 Knockout Stage: Oct 28 - Nov 2 Grand Final & Opening Ceremony: Nov 9 Web: X: Facebook: Instagram: TikTok: Meta Threads: Whatsapp: Download League of Legends now: [...] We will immediately go over to the draw show just as an explanation. Three bowls on stage right now. The teams are grouped together in the bowls consisting of their record. The first bowl is the two teams...

evidences.title: WORLDS 2025 LPL TEAMS BREAKDOWN - MAJOR REGION ...
evidences.content: have probably Top Esports and Anyone's Legend who are more methodical. Top Esports the most likely team to take an objective area, blackout vision, win through that, more LCK style play. 369 on tanks. Kanavi, really the heart and soul of this team, and we have JackeyLove, maybe with his last dance ever. JackeyLove's talked about his health issues. This might be the last year we see JackeyLove in pro play. What a career he's had. Top eSports, the more methodical side of things. Anyone's legend in there too. I think they more play around their jungle, support. You know about them. It's not too dissimilar from Anyone's Legend from MSI. I think that individual playerform, their drafts and their objective play has taken a hit from MSI though, they need to get snappy on this meta. If they don't [...] though, they need to get snappy on this meta. If they don't catch up soon, I'm worried for them. I think that Flandre, Shanks and a little bit of Kael as well have struggled individually. They need to step up to the plate. Then of course we have Invictus Gaming well and truly a dice roll, a wild card. Entirely vibes based, changed out Jiejie for Wei mid way through the year and the chemis...

evidences.title: T1's historic win: The top 10 moments that defined Worlds 2024
evidences.content: 09 ## T1 claim historic fifth Worlds title In one of the greatest accomplishments in League of Legends history, T1 won back-to-back Worlds titles for the second time and this time did it with the exact same roster as 2023. Image 14: T1 pictured lifting the trophy after winning the 2024 League of Legends World Championships in London, UK. T1 maintained their status as LoL's greatest team with 2024's Worlds title © Riot Games It was a third-consecutive Worlds final for the iconic squad and they once again performed when it mattered most following a difficult domestic season that saw them barely qualify for the international tournament as Korea's fourth seeds. [...] © Riot Games Reigning LPL champions BLG almost didn’t even make it to the Knockouts after struggling in the Swiss Stages. However, the team recovered brilliantly and eventually made it all the way to the final in an impressive turnaround. In the grand final, we saw the extreme highs that were expected of this talented squad as they stood toe to toe with T1 and came agonizingly close to defeating what is possibly the greatest LoL roster ever assembled. evidences.title: Everything YOU Need to Know About Worlds 2025 - YouTube
evidences.content: played in game one are banned for game two. The 20 champions in game 1 and two are banned for game three, and so on. For the Swiss stage, it works the same as it did in 2023 and 2024. The 16 teams are all given a seed based on how they performed in their region, with winners given the one seed, runner-ups the two seed, and third place the three seed. Depending on who wins the playin match, either the LCK or LPL3 seed will be promoted to two seed for this withdraw. This way, there are five one seeds, six two seeds, and five three seeds. In round one, one seeds will be drawn against three seeds, and two seeds will play against other two seeds in a best of one. The way Swiss works is this. If you win a match, you move up to 1 and 0. And if you lose, you go to 0 and one. Every team who has [...] two best performing regions at MSI, those being the LCK and LPL. So, we have four LCK, four LPL, three LCP, three LEC, and three LTA teams at the tournament. The matches will start on October 14th at 4 a.m. EST. Other time zones will be shown on screen. This match will be between the LCK 4 seed and LPL 4 seed, so that the main event will have just 16 teams in it. If you watched prior World C...

evidences.title: Split 3: LPL Standings - LoL Esports
evidences.content: ###### Playoffs Play-In Knights Rival Eight teams are seeded into 4 qualifying best of 5 matches. The winner from each of these matches qualify for Playoffs. ###### Playoffs Eight teams come together for a double elimination bracket with the top team qualifying for Worlds. ###### Regional Finals The top four teams in Championship Points participate in a seeded elimination bracket. The winner of the opening match (first seed against the second) qualifies for Worlds. The loser plays against the winner of second series (third seed against the fourth) for the last spot at Worlds. ##### Participating Teams All teams in the region participate in this event TOP ESPORTS TES FunPlus Phoenix FPX BILIBILI GAMING BLG Invictus Gaming IG Anyone's Legend AL Xi'an Team WE WE [...] ## Split 3: LPL Standings Jul – Sept ##### Split 3 overview The last regional Split, with each region running their own Split format and style. evidences.title: Fnatic's possibilities for the rest of Worlds - The Game Haus
evidences.content: The big deciding factor in this match-up would be top lane. Huni is such a wildcard. It is hard to tell if he will be able to stomp Soaz and solo carry, or become a liability. SKT will also bring two junglers, so if Peanut or Blank fails to stuff Broxah, then they have a back-up. Fnatic’s top and jungle will be put to the test, yet again. ### EDG EDG is at the head of the table for Group A as China’s first seed. This is almost certain to be a jungle-mid contest. Clearlove is one of the most touted junglers appearing on the Worlds stage and Scout throws some serious fast balls. Both of these players enjoy similar champion pools to Caps and Broxah, as well. [...] Fnatic’s best bet is to continue drafting towards these compositions. Putting Soaz on Cho’Gath or carries, such as Rumble, Jarvan IV, etc. is not ideal. Broxah has historically performed well on Elise, but when Fnatic does not close the game in less than 40 minutes the pick becomes useless. Jesiz is in a similar boat, where he can perform on Braum, Thresh, and even Camille support, but the meta strongly favors enchanter supports who can abuse the strength of Ardent Censer. ## Possibility #1: Group A Group A consists of ED...

evidences.title: 2025 World Championship
evidences.content: The page describes the tournament format for the 2025 World Championship, including a Swiss Stage with sixteen teams playing a five-round Swiss format where teams with three wins advance and teams with three losses are eliminated. It also notes qualification rules, including regional seeds and a Play-In match for additional slots.
